evidence: Docstring 'Original Hall's conjecture with exponent 1/2' under the module header referencing Wikipedia's Hall's conjecture page.
Docstring: 'Danilov proved that one cannot replace the exponent 1/2 with larger number' — a solved sharpness result about Hall's conjecture, not the conjecture itself.
Docstring: 'Weak form of Hall's conjecture: relax the exponent from 1/2 to 1/2 - epsilon' — a variant, not the exact conjecture.
Module header '# First Hardy–Littlewood conjecture' with the Wikipedia reference; the decl states the prime-constellation asymptotic pi_P(n) ~ C_P integral.
Section '## Second Hardy-Littlewood Conjecture' with its Wikipedia URL comment; the decl states pi(x+y) <= pi(x) + pi(y) for x, y >= 2.
Docstring: 'where pi(z) denotes the prime-counting function, giving the number of primes up to and including z'.
Docstring: 'Richards [Ri74] showed that only one of the two Hardy-Littlewood conjectures can be true'.
Docstring: 'Richards [Ri74] showed that only one of the two Hardy-Littlewood conjectures can be true' — the incompatibility statement involves the second conjecture as hypothesis target.

Module header 'Idoneal numbers completeness conjecture' asserts the list of 65 idoneal numbers is complete, referencing Wikipedia's Idoneal number page; no Wikidata item exists for the completeness conjecture itself.
Docstring '**Inscribed square problem** Does every Jordan curve admit an inscribed square?' matches the module header's Wikipedia reference to the inscribed square problem (Toeplitz conjecture).
Module header: 'every Jordan curve (i.e. simple close curve in R²)' — the statement quantifies over embeddings of the circle, i.e. Jordan curves.

Docstring '**Inscribed rectangle problem** Does every Jordan curve admit inscribed rectangles of any given aspect ratio?' — an aspect-ratio variant of the inscribed square problem, not the square statement itself.
The statement quantifies over Jordan curves (embeddings Circle -> R²), per the module header's 'every Jordan curve' phrasing.
Docstring 'It is known that every Jordan curve admits at least one inscribed rectangle' — a solved variant listed under the inscribed square problem reference.
Docstring 'every *smooth* Jordan curve admits inscribed rectangles of all aspect ratios' — a solved smooth-case variant of the inscribed square problem.
Docstring 'every C² Jordan curve admits an inscribed square' — the solved C² case of the inscribed square problem, not the full open conjecture.

Module header '# Invariant Subspace Problem' with Wikipedia reference; the decl states every bounded operator on a separable complex Hilbert space of dim >= 2 has a non-trivial closed invariant subspace.
Docstring: 'every bounded linear operator T : H -> H on a separable Hilbert space H of dimension at least 2'.
Solved finite-dimensional case: 'Every (bounded) linear operator T on a finite-dimensional linear space H of dimension at least 2 has a non-trivial (closed) T-invariant subspace'.
Solved non-separable case: 'Every bounded linear operator T on a non-separable Hilbert space H has a non-trivial closed T-invariant subspace'.
Solved normal-operator case: 'Every normal linear operator T on a Hilbert space H of dimension at least 2 has a non-trivial closed T-invariant subspace'.
Read/Enflo counterexample on l1: 'There exists a bounded linear operator T on the l1 space ... without non-trivial closed T-invariant subspace [Read 1985]'.
